chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Now let's understand how the frontend works. The frontend is primarily made up of 3 languages. HTML, CSS and JavaScript. These are the 3 languages that are needed if you are making a website.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Every website needs HTML, CSS, JavaScript in most of the cases. If you only use HTML, then it becomes a basic skeleton of the website. If you want to style a page, then you can style it.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: This button is red. This button is curvy. These edges are made up of CSS. CSS is used to style a page. CSS is used to style a page.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: CSS is used to style a page. CSS is the beauty of HTML page. HTML is the body of any page. This is a standard which is agreed upon. Every website is made up of HTML

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: so that the browser knows how to parse it. The browser parses the HTML. HTML is a skeleton. Every website is made up of HTML. This is a standard.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: This standard is made so that the browser knows how to parse it. If you have learned 80% of HTML, then you are good to go for web development. There are some things that can be done with HTML.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: This is called client-side scripting. If we want to code in the client, only on the client side, we can do it with the help of JavaScript. This is called beauty with brain.

chunks.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
chunks.text: Beauty added from CSS. Brain came from JavaScript. JavaScript helps you to make the page dynamic. If you want to change the content without loading the page, all this work is done with JavaScript.

chunks.text: There are a lot of mosquitoes here. Let me kill them. My racket is working. Like the video. Learn 100% HTML first.
